signals.title: New York Times says OpenAI hid evidence in ChatGPT copyright trial
signals.desc: News publishers say OpenAI hid tools and datasets that could identify copyrighted journalism in ChatGPT outputs, escalating their lawsuit with a new motion for sanctions.

signals.title: OpenAI may have made a fatal misstep in copyright fight with news orgs
signals.desc: OpenAI may be sanctioned for hiding, deleting ChatGPT logs in NYT copyright fight.

signals.title: Fidji Simo steps down from OpenAI’s no. 2 role
signals.desc: OpenAI's No. 2 executive, Fidji Simo, is stepping down from her full-time role after her medical leave proved longer than expected — a leadership vacuum that comes at a tricky time as the company eyes a possible IPO and races to catch Anthropic in the enterprise market.

signals.title: GPT-5.6 is now the preferred model in Microsoft 365 Copilot
signals.desc: Learn how GPT-5.6 powers Microsoft 365 Copilot with stronger AI capabilities across Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Chat, and Cowork for faster, higher-quality work.

signals.title: Paris-based AI voice startup Gradium raises $100M seed, backed by Nvidia
signals.desc: The company is using the cash to open an office in the Bay Area and compete for talent there, "strengthening its position at the heart of the world's leading AI ecosystem."

signals.title: OpenAI launches its new family of models with GPT-5.6
signals.desc: OpenAI's latest family of models promises improvements across a range of areas, including cybersecurity.

signals.title: The ChatGPT browser is already dead
signals.desc: OpenAI is already shutting down ChatGPT Atlas, its browser that could do tasks for you on your behalf, less than a year after launching it. Atlas was announced in October, but as part of its wave of news about ChatGPT Work today, the company confirmed that it will be "sunsetting" Atlas and is targeting an […]

signals.title: Elon Musk praises Mythos/Fable, promises not to ‘cut off’ Anthropic
signals.desc: Should Anthropic trust Elon Musk to host its models? With about $40 billion in revenue at stake, Musk insists that the company can.

signals.title: Anthropic found a hidden space where Claude puzzles over concepts
signals.desc: The AI firm Anthropic has developed a technique that has given it the clearest glimpse yet at what’s really going on inside large language models as they answer questions or carry out tasks. What they found ranges from the mundane to the unnerving. Researchers at the company built a tool called the Jacobian lens (or…

signals.title: Nvidia is a victim of the compute marketplace it created
signals.desc: Having proven how valuable compute can be, the company finds itself at the center of a market everyone wants to be in — while simpler technologies and less interesting companies get rich on the sidelines.

signals.title: Anthropic Wants You to Pay Up for Claude Fable 5
signals.desc: Claude subscribers must soon pay usage-based fees to access Anthropic’s best consumer AI model—a sign that the golden era of AI subscriptions is ending.

signals.title: How did the government decide OpenAI’s frontier model was safe to release?
signals.desc: "Exactly what that dialog looked like between the government and Anthropic and OpenAI is unclear."

signals.title: OpenAI rolls out GPT-5.6 after government greenlight — and announces ‘ChatGPT Work’
signals.desc: About two weeks after OpenAI's GPT-5.6 was caught up in regulatory drama - rolled out only to government-approved organizations during a "limited preview" period - the company has received the Trump administration's greenlight for a public rollout of the model. OpenAI CEO Sam Altman called it "the best model we have ever produced." To celebrate, […]

signals.title: Anthropic’s new Claude feature is quietly selling you on AI
signals.desc: Claude’s new Reflect dashboard doesn’t just visualize how you use AI. It also subtly reinforces how much of your daily work now depends on Anthropic’s chatbot.

signals.title: Anthropic, OpenAI, and SpaceX are bigger than the last 25 years of tech exits
signals.desc: Three big AI IPOs are set to generate more value than all the U.S. VC-backed exits since 2000.

signals.title: ChatGPT is now a partner for your most ambitious work
signals.desc: ChatGPT Work is an agent that can take action across your apps and files, stay with a project for hours if needed, and turn a goal into finished work.

signals.title: Say hello to Claude Wrapped
signals.desc: The popularity of Spotify Wrapped has kicked off a wide range of year-in-review features, on apps from YouTube to Uber - and now, the lookback trend has come to AI. Anthropic on Thursday announced a "reflect" feature for its Claude chatbot, allowing users to see an analysis of their usage data over the past month, […]

signals.title: GPT-5.5 Bio Bug Bounty
signals.desc: Details about the OpenAI Bio Bounty program

signals.title: GPT-5.6: Frontier intelligence that scales with your ambition
signals.desc: More intelligence from every token, stronger performance per dollar, and more capability on demand for your hardest work.

signals.title: Popular open source AI developer tool Ollama raises $65M, grows to nearly 9M users
signals.desc: Benchmark-backed Ollama has amassed 176,000 stars, and nearly 17,000 forks on GitHub by helping developers easily run AI on their PCs.

signals.title: Lawsuit: Man used Grok to make 7K sex images of stepdaughter, then shot himself
signals.desc: More young girls sue X over Grok CSAM; X accused of shielding child predators.

signals.title: Google updates Android Bench with new LLMs, but Gemini still lags behind
signals.desc: Android Bench is evolving, and developers can help guide that process.

signals.title: This Former DeepMind Exec Thinks the AI Arms Race Could End in Disaster
signals.desc: Verity Harding tells WIRED that the US government’s nationalistic attitude toward AI is evidence that a worst-case scenario is taking shape.

signals.title: An AI agent startup just let its agent run its $100M fundraise
signals.desc: Lyzr, a startup that builds AI agents for enterprises, used its own AI agent to raise a $100 million round — proof, evidently, that the product actually works.

signals.title: Introducing GPT-Live
signals.desc: A new generation of voice models for natural human-AI interaction, now powering ChatGPT Voice.

signals.title: Microsoft’s carbon emissions went up 25 percent last year
signals.desc: Microsoft may once again be struggling to keep up with its own climate goals, according to its 2026 sustainability report. As reported by GeekWire, the report states that Microsoft's carbon emissions increased 25 percent in 2025, totalling 34 million metric tons "without select interventions." Microsoft says this was "driven primarily by the expansion of our […]

signals.title: Fidji Simo steps down from leading OpenAI’s AGI work due to illness
signals.desc: OpenAI's Fidji Simo is departing her full-time role as the company's AGI chief and is transitioning to being a "part-time advisor," she said on X. The news follows Simo's original announcement in April that she would take a few weeks of medical leave due to a neuroimmune condition, shortly after she had taken on the […]

signals.title: OpenAI is shutting down Atlas, but its AI browser ambitions are still growing
signals.desc: OpenAI is sunsetting its AI-powered browser after less than a year. But it's moving some agentic browsing features to its desktop app and a Chrome extension.

signals.title: OpenAI’s CEO of AGI Deployment, Fidji Simo, Is Stepping Down
signals.desc: The move comes after Simo took significant medical leave. She will stay on as a part-time adviser.

signals.title: Can AI answer the $3 trillion question?
signals.desc: The AI ROI debate has returned and the numbers are even bigger, as are, perhaps, the consequences.

signals.title: OpenAI wants its new tool to do your work for you and with you
signals.desc: Rebranded Codex promises independent workflows that can run "for hours if needed."

signals.title: Humanoid robots controlled by surgeons did world-first operation on live pigs
signals.desc: Preclinical trial is testing the feasibility of humanoid robots in surgery.

signals.title: Google will now tell you if an ad was made with AI
signals.desc: You can see if ads on Google Search, Google Discover, and YouTube were made or edited using AI from a new section in Google's "My Ad Center," as reported earlier by TechCrunch. The update, announced on Thursday, adds a "created or edited with AI" label under the "how this ad was made" tab. Users can […]

signals.title: Meta enters the crowded AI coding battle with Muse Spark 1.1
signals.desc: Meta's pitch to users is Spark's ability to handle large agentic workloads, fix bugs, and help with large code migrations — the kind of automation that enterprises are increasingly turning to AI companies to provide.

signals.title: Google will now disclose which ads are made with AI
signals.desc: While Google prohibits misleading and deceptive ads, an ad can still leverage AI to create some type of synthetic or digitally altered content. Until now, that's something Google only required election ads to disclose.

signals.title: Facing US export controls, China's DeepSeek plans to make its own chips
signals.desc: It's early, but the plan is to reduce dependency on Nvidia and Huawei.

signals.title: Instagram users: Here’s how to stop Meta’s AI from using your photos
signals.desc: Muse Image allows users to generate AI images using photos from public Instagram accounts. As long as a person's profile is public, another user can tag that account and use their images as part of an AI-generated creation.

signals.title: Meta’s new AI chips will begin production in September
signals.desc: The company is taking a modular approach to designing these chips, anticipating that their needs will change as AI evolves rapidly by the time the chips are in production.

signals.title: The 1X Neo Robot Has Freaky Fast Fingers
signals.desc: The soft, oddly intimate home-chore robot has been given some very tactile hands.

signals.title: Microsoft’s patch Tuesdays are about to get bigger
signals.desc: Windows 11 updates could soon include fixes for more security issues at once. Microsoft said in a blog post on Thursday that it's now using AI to "identify potential issues earlier," which means "customers will see a higher volume of security updates included in each security release." Hackers, even amateurs, have increasingly been using AI […]

breakthroughs.p: A new analysis from OpenAI reveals issues in SWE-Bench Pro, a popular coding benchmark, raising concerns about reliability and accuracy in evaluating AI models.

breakthroughs.h: How AI could enable autonomous robot workers in workplaces—and maybe homes
breakthroughs.p: Top robotics researchers and founders explain how robot autonomy is evolving.
Introducing GeneBench-Pro, a new benchmark testing AI performance in genomics, biology, and scientific research using complex, real-world datasets.
A new OpenAI research paper shows how AI agents are transforming work, enabling longer, more complex tasks and expanding productivity across roles.

breakthroughs.h: How GPT-5 helped immunologist Derya Unutmaz solve a 3-year-old mystery
breakthroughs.p: GPT-5 Pro helped solve a 3-year-old immunology mystery, offering insights into T cell behavior. The breakthrough could support cancer and autoimmune research.
